Summary: QA the Arabic version for accuracy, tone, and bilingual consistency
Description:
Domain: Content & SEO
Issue: TGTM-917 — QA the Arabic version for accuracy, tone, and bilingual consistency
Level: Task
Hierarchy path: TGTM-10 — Create Dubai tokenized real estate education hub > TGTM-116 — Localize priority content into Arabic > TGTM-917 — QA the Arabic version for accuracy, tone, and bilingual consistency

Original intent:
Perform a final review of the Arabic localized content to verify translation accuracy, tone alignment, terminology consistency, and parity with the English education hub.

How we are doing this:
Use a content-operations workflow: clarify the audience (Arabic-speaking investors, developers, and regional stakeholders), confirm the user question/search intent, prepare the brief or source pack, create the asset, route it through editorial/SEO/compliance/localization review as needed, publish or activate through Arabic education hub pages, bilingual landing pages, localized social/email snippets, then measure performance and feed learnings back into the content calendar.

Preliminary solution:
Use a publication QA checklist covering copy accuracy, links, mobile rendering, metadata, CTA behavior, tracking tags, Arabic/English parity where relevant, and compliance-approved wording before marking complete.

Deliverable:
Arabic localized content, bilingual glossary updates, QA notes, and parity confirmation against the English source.

Dependencies / inputs:
Approved Blocks Estate positioning, tone of voice, and compliance-safe terminology; Bilingual glossary, English source copy, Arabic reviewer, and regional tone guidance.

Implementation notes:
- Keep the language educational and practical; avoid hype, legal certainty, guaranteed returns, or automatic-liquidity claims.
- For Arabic content, use Modern Standard Arabic with regionally natural business language and maintain bilingual meaning parity.

Acceptance criteria:
- Deliverable is completed in the correct format and linked back to the parent Story objective.
- Content is reviewed for accuracy, brand tone, compliance-safe language, and user clarity.
- Completion evidence is available: draft/link/file/checklist/approval note/status update.
- Arabic version preserves source meaning, uses approved terminology, and passes bilingual QA.
- Published page or final asset renders correctly on desktop/mobile and all links/CTAs/tracking work.
Parent summary: Localize priority content into Arabic
